description: Batch 006 operational source-to-Lean live-field map for Gaussian/root/Hessian/activity/H# obligations in THE-ERIKSSON-PROGRAMME. These entries are navigation metadata, not primary mathematical evidence.

repo_operational_crosswalk.title: LLM-facing source-to-Lean dependency crosswalk for THE-ERIKSSON-PROGRAMME
repo_operational_crosswalk.author: Generated source-db batch 002
repo_operational_crosswalk.license_note: Repository metadata only; not a mathematical source. Use to navigate primary source entries, not as proof.

citations.summary: Batch 006 live-field map for the raw-source M3 frontier: Gaussian pushforward, covariance/root certificate, Hessian, activity construction, support, pointwise decay, Appendix-F/H# and flow remain ordered obligations.
extracted_claims: BalabanCMP116SourceAssumptions explicitly lists separate fields for covariance_root_certificate, root_localization, gaussian_pushforward, wilson_hessian_identification, local_physical_activity_construction, support, measurability, raw_pointwise_decay, Appendix-F geometry, rooted_hsharp_remainder_identity, amplitude bounds, coupling flow and IR.
These fields should not be discharged by one monolithic source citation; each needs its own source target or theorem package.
The correct LLM route is topological/order-aware: first dictionary/covariance/root, then Gaussian pushforward, then Hessian/activity, then termwise decay, then Appendix-F H# and final Rsc/flow/IR.

formulas.ascii: covariance_root_certificate -> root_localization -> gaussian_pushforward -> wilson_hessian_identification -> local_physical_activity_construction -> support/measurable -> raw_pointwise_decay -> AppendixF/Hsharp -> flow/IR
formulas.conclusion: Prevents agents from using final H# or flow statements to backfill upstream Gaussian/root/Hessian obligations.

formulas.statement: Guard against a monolithic source theorem.
formulas.ascii: one source-looking package != proof of all raw_source fields
formulas.conclusion: Every field must either cite a primary formula or remain a live hypothesis.

use_for: Entry point for agents choosing the next source-backed M3 frontier theorem.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this operational card as mathematical evidence.
Do not collapse separate source fields into one broad assumption.
open_questions: Exact source dictionary for physical fluctuation coordinates and covariance/root variables.
Exact Wilson Hessian identification in the repository dictionary.
Source theorem identifying local physicalActivity with the finite CMP116 H(Z) resummation.
Termwise norm estimate and raw_pointwise_decay in physical gauge types.

citations.summary: Live-field card for the finite/physical covariance-root certificate feeding the raw-source Gaussian interface.
extracted_claims: CMP116 (2.5)-(2.6) is already the product-Gaussian pushforward anchor but leaves the coordinate dictionary and determinant/Jacobian normalization open.
Dimock II (271)-(276) gives a useful resolvent/root architecture in a scalar model; it is a guide for the certificate shape, not a Yang-Mills source proof.
The Lean target separates covariance/root certificate from gaussian_pushforward and from root_localization.

formulas.ascii: PhysicalLocalizedCovarianceRootCertificate precision covariance root covNormBound rootNormBound covWeight rootWeight
Dimock II covariance-root resolvent != CMP116 Yang-Mills covariance-root proof
formulas.conclusion: Forces a source/dictionary theorem before using Dimock-style formulas in the physical Lean types.

use_for: Planning a focused source extraction for the covariance/root certificate.
do_not_use_for: Do not use product Gaussian pushforward alone as proof of the covariance-root certificate.
open_questions: Exact operator equality/inequality fields required by PhysicalLocalizedCovarianceRootCertificate.
Uniform constants and physical bond weights in the repository's PhysicalBond convention.
Coordinate dictionary from Balaban's covariance/root variables into PhysicalGaugeOneCochain.

citations.summary: Live-field card for the Gaussian pushforward equality after the covariance-root change of variables.
extracted_claims: The existing citation key cmp116.gaussian-pushforward.2.5-2.6 is visually confirmed as the anchor for moving to the product standard Gaussian interface.
The open obligations are the source-to-Lean coordinate dictionary and determinant/Jacobian normalization, not the visual existence of the displayed pushforward step.
This field feeds gaussian_pushforward separately from root_localization and Hessian identification.

PhysicalGaugeCMP116Dictionary.CMP116GaussianPushforwardNormalization.of_sourceNormalizedChange is the Lean endpoint that packages the coordinate-map equality, physical-Gaussian equality and normalized pushforward identity into the structured Gaussian normalization record.

PhysicalGaugeCMP116Dictionary.CMP116GaussianPushforwardNormalization.of_sourceRecords assembles the structured record from the independent source records CMP116GaussianCoordinateMapSource, CMP116GaussianPhysicalLawSource and CMP116GaussianNormalizedPushforwardSource.

PhysicalGaugeCMP116LocalizedGaussianActivitySourceHypotheses.of_sourceRecords and PhysicalGaugeCMP116LocalizedGaussianRawActivitySourceHypotheses.of_sourceRecords now let localized and raw source packages consume those three records directly.

formulas.ascii: (balabanCMP116Dmu0 (Cube d L) lieDim).map ((D t k).gaussianRootMap (root t k)) = physicalGaussian t k
Jacobian/determinant normalization must be stated or packaged
formulas.conclusion: Prevents agents from declaring pushforward closure from the display alone.

formulas.ascii: sourceCoordinateMap = D.gaussianRootMap root; sourcePhysicalGaussian = physicalGaussian; (balabanCMP116Dmu0 (Cube d L) lieDim).map sourceCoordinateMap = sourcePhysicalGaussian
CMP116GaussianCoordinateMapSource; CMP116GaussianPhysicalLawSource; CMP116GaussianNormalizedPushforwardSource
formulas.conclusion: Feeds PhysicalGaugeCMP116Dictionary.CMP116GaussianPushforwardNormalization.of_sourceRecords and the localized/raw of_sourceRecords constructors.

use_for: Prompting a narrow theorem that discharges only gaussian_pushforward.
do_not_use_for: Do not use this card to discharge covariance_root_certificate or root_localization.
open_questions: Exact finite-dimensional coordinate dictionary for fluctuation fields.
Normalization/Jacobian theorem compatible with balabanCMP116Dmu0.
Measurability assumptions for the map in the repository type stack.

citations.summary: Live-field card for root localization: local pieces, resolvent/random-walk control and agreement with the physical activity support relation.
extracted_claims: CMP116 (2.7)-(2.10) localizes the product-Gaussian expression into H(Z,Z0) and H(Z), but the current citation explicitly does not prove exact finite root-piece reconstruction.
Dimock II gives a useful covariance/root localization architecture, but it remains a scalar analogy unless transported through the YM dictionary.
Root localization must be discharged before using localized activity construction as a physical support theorem.

formulas.ascii: rootLocalization t k
localized H(Z) display != exact finite root-piece reconstruction theorem
formulas.conclusion: Keeps approximation/truncation and exact source equality separate.

use_for: Designing the next root-localization source package.
do_not_use_for: Do not turn Dimock scalar covariance-root localization into a YM theorem without a dictionary.
open_questions: Exact local root-piece theorem or declared rootLocalization proposition.
Bounded support/enlargement convention matching physicalActiveSupport.
Uniform constants for rootWeight and decay.

citations.summary: Live-field card for identifying Balaban's second variation/Hessian object with the repository Wilson-Hessian interface.
extracted_claims: BalabanCMP116SourceAssumptions carries wilson_hessian_identification as a separate Prop-valued source field.
No existing CMP116 Lemma-3 citation should be used to backfill this field unless it contains the exact Hessian dictionary statement.
This identification should sit after the physical dictionary and before local activity construction/termwise estimates.

formulas.ascii: wilsonHessianIdentification t k
dictionary + Hessian identity before activity termwise estimates
formulas.conclusion: Keeps analytic residual estimates tied to the correct physical operator.

use_for: Preventing accidental discharge of Hessian identity from activity decay estimates.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite Lemma 3 final bound as Hessian identification.
open_questions: Exact source locator for Wilson action Hessian expansion in the Balaban/CMP116 stack.
Dictionary theorem from source lattice gauge variables to PhysicalGaugeField/PhysicalBond.
Sign and normalization conventions for second variation.

citations.summary: Live-field card for constructing the physical local activity from Balaban's localized H(Z) expansion.
extracted_claims: CMP116 (2.7)-(2.10) anchors the existence of localized H(Z,Z0) and H(Z) with component factorization.
The repository field local_physical_activity_construction is separate from the final Lemma 3 bound and from support containment.
The construction must identify the finite resummation index stack with PhysicalGaugeLocalActivity.globalEval before termwise estimates can be used.

formulas.ascii: localActivityConstruction t k
(physicalActivity t k Z).globalEval psi phi = balabanCMP116H (R t k) Z psi phi

use_for: Splitting construction from decay and support obligations.
do_not_use_for: Do not use final Lemma 3 estimate as proof of the construction identity.
open_questions: Exact map from source localization domains to OmegaPolymerType supports.
Finite index dictionary D/P/Z0/Z0' for H(Z).
Component factorization theorem compatible with repository summands.

citations.summary: Live-field card for support containment and measurability of the physical local activity.
extracted_claims: BalabanCMP116SourceAssumptions separates spectator_support_subset, fluctuation_support_subset and activity_stronglyMeasurable from activity construction and raw decay.
Support fields must use the repository's physicalActiveSupport and PhysicalGaugeCMP116Dictionary physicalBondsOfCells/skeleton conventions.
This card prevents source locality statements from being treated as measurability theorems.

formulas.ascii: (physicalActivity t k X).spectatorSupport subset physicalActiveSupport t k X
(physicalActivity t k X).fluctuationSupport subset physicalActiveSupport t k X
StronglyMeasurable fun xi => adapted activity globalEval psi xi

use_for: Organizing locality/support tasks after activity construction.
do_not_use_for: Do not infer support subset from decay alone.
open_questions: Exact enlargement convention from source localized domains to physicalActiveSupport.
Measurability proof for the adapted activity in CMP116FluctuationField.
Dictionary between source active sets and skeleton HF X.val.

citations.summary: Live-field card for pointwise termwise estimates and the final raw_pointwise_decay field.
extracted_claims: The finite resummation theorem consumes termwise estimates and a summed-weight budget; it does not prove the source termwise estimate.

BalabanCMP116SourceAssumptions.raw_pointwise_decay is pointwise in t,k,X and physical fields, while CMP116Lemma3ActivityTermwiseScaleBoundary.termwise_estimate is indexed by flattened D/P/Z0/Z0' summands.
Eq. (2.29), Eq. (2.31), Eq. (2.37) and activity identification must be composed before claiming raw pointwise decay.

formulas.ascii: norm (R.summand Z D P Z0 Z0' psi phi) <= R.termWeight Z D P Z0 Z0'
norm ((physicalActivity t k X).globalEval psi phi) <= amplitude t k * weight t k X

use_for: Preventing agents from calling finite-sum norm lemmas analytic source estimates.
do_not_use_for: Do not claim raw_pointwise_decay from the final finite-sum bridge alone.
open_questions: Source termwise estimate for the actual CMP116 summand.
Identification of pResidualWeight/postPSourceWeight with source formulas.
Completion of Eq. (2.29), Eq. (2.31), Eq. (2.37) source dictionaries.

citations.summary: Live-field card for the rooted H# identity tying the raw-source scale family to the scalar Rsc frontier.
extracted_claims: BalabanCMP116SourceAssumptions.rooted_hsharp_remainder_identity is a separate field after rawSource is constructed from Gaussian/root/Hessian/activity/decay components.
Dimock Appendix F provides the H# architecture and loss 3*kappa0+3, but it must be connected to the physical Yang-Mills raw source scale family.
This identity should not be used to backfill Gaussian pushforward or local activity construction.

formulas.ascii: Rsc t k = tsum_{P : OmegaPolymerType with r in skeleton P} Re (balabanCMP116AppendixFHsharpOfIntegratedKsharp ... P)
rawSource components before rooted H# remainder identity
formulas.conclusion: Keeps H# identity as downstream composition, not upstream proof.

use_for: Planning the handoff from local activities to Rsc/rooted H# frontiers.
do_not_use_for: Do not use H# identity as evidence for gaussian_pushforward, root_localization or Hessian identity.
open_questions: Exact source theorem for the rooted H# representation in the Yang-Mills stack.
Proof that Appendix-F holes relation uses skeleton/Omega connectivity in repository types.
Summability and real-part normalization of the rooted series.

citations.summary: Commit-sequence card for discharging the Gaussian/root/Hessian/activity stack without false downstream progress.
extracted_claims: Recommended ordering: source dictionary for covariance/root -> gaussian pushforward normalization -> root localization -> Wilson Hessian dictionary -> local activity construction -> support/measurable -> termwise estimate -> H# identity.
Each commit should remove one live field or sharpen a source target; new wrappers are cosmetic unless they discharge a named field.
The source-db should be updated in the same commit with citation keys, local artifact paths, and open questions.

formulas.ascii: one commit = one field discharged or one source target sharpened
formulas.conclusion: Keeps reviewable progress and prevents broad unverifiable claims.

formulas.ascii: covroot -> pushforward -> rootLocalization -> Hessian -> activityConstruction -> support/measurable -> termwise -> Hsharp
formulas.conclusion: Preferred work queue for the raw-source M3 stack.

use_for: Guiding source and Lean commit order.
do_not_use_for: Do not add more package constructors unless they consume one listed field.
open_questions: Which exact source page proves each field.
Whether fields can be bundled without hiding dependencies.
Verification commands for focused olean builds and oracle checks.

citations.summary: Guard card: final bounds, Appendix-F architecture and Dimock analogies cannot backfill upstream Gaussian/root/Hessian/source-dictionary fields.
extracted_claims: The repository already distinguishes visual source anchors, source-pending entries and theorem-fed consumers; Batch 006 reinforces that distinction for raw-source fields.
Final Lemma 3/H# estimates are downstream consumers, not proofs of Gaussian pushforward, covariance root, Hessian identity or physical activity construction.
Dimock I-III are extracted source architecture but not direct Yang-Mills source closure.

formulas.ascii: downstream estimate cannot prove upstream dictionary/root/Hessian fields
formulas.conclusion: Hard guard for LLM source reasoning.

formulas.ascii: Dimock scalar RG architecture can guide theorem shape but not substitute YM source proof
formulas.conclusion: Prevents category errors in future agents.

use_for: Automated checks by future LLMs before claiming source closure.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this as source evidence; it is a guard.
open_questions: Exact source extraction for each upstream field.
Potential comparison theorem between Dimock-style scalar objects and repository physical objects, if desired.
